name: fanbasis
one_liner: FanBasis is full-stack business infrastructure for internet creators/entrepreneurs, unifying payments, checkout, subscriptions, courses/community, and CRM, with a REST API for payment and commerce operations.
access_notes: FanBasis accounts are free to open on a revenue-share model (no upfront fee), and the API exposes a sandbox at qa.dev-fan-basis.com for test credentials. API keys are provisioned from the account/dashboard and the developer docs portal (dev-docs.fanbasis.com) is login-gated. The enterprises page pushes a sales/discovery call, so whether every self-serve account can immediately generate live API keys vs. needing onboarding is not fully confirmed from public docs.
api_notes: REST API under /public-api/ with production (www.fanbasis.com) and sandbox (qa.dev-fan-basis.com) base URLs. Documented operations cover Checkout Sessions (create/retrieve/delete/transactions), Customers & Payments (list, payment methods, charge), Subscriptions (create/track/cancel/extend + proration), Discount Codes, Transactions (fees/payouts), and Refunds & Disputes. Standard response envelope, pagination via page/per_page (max 100). Official SDKs advertised for Node, Python, PHP, Ruby plus a @fanbasis/checkout-sdk npm package; Postman collection available.
mcp_notes: No official FanBasis-vendor MCP server found. Search surfaces a third-party Zapier 'FanBasis MCP' (Zapier-hosted, not vendor) and an unrelated 'FanBase' MCP by XBorg (a different creator/CRM product). No MCP shipped by FanBasis itself.
main_blocker: Developer docs portal is login-gated and API keys are provisioned from an account dashboard; unclear whether all self-serve accounts get instant live API access or require sales onboarding (enterprises page emphasizes a discovery call). A sandbox exists, so building/testing is feasible once an account is created.

evidence.claim: FanBasis API uses API key auth via x-api-key header, has production and sandbox (qa.dev-fan-basis.com) base URLs, REST endpoints for checkout sessions, customers/payments, subscriptions, discount codes, transactions, refunds & disputes, and HMAC-SHA256 signed webhooks with 13 event types.
FanBasis positions itself as full-stack business infrastructure unifying payments, fulfillment, course/community hosting, CRM and operations for internet entrepreneurs.
Developer API docs portal requires sign-in ('Sign in to access API Docs'), indicating gated documentation access.
@fanbasis/checkout-sdk is a published npm package providing server-side access to the FanBasis Checkout REST API.

open_questions: Can any free self-serve FanBasis account generate live API keys instantly, or is live API access gated behind sales onboarding/enterprise plans? What is the exact process to obtain sandbox vs production keys, and are there plan-tier restrictions on API usage?
